_note: Flavour descriptions for languages, so every tongue on a sheet carries context — not just the SRD's terse script/speaker data. Keyed by lower-cased language name. Used as the PRIMARY description; the engine falls back to the SRD catalog (type/script/speakers) for anything not here, and to a player-written description for bespoke flavour tongues (e.g. Kenderspeak). Edition-shared.
common: The trade tongue of the realms — nearly everyone speaks at least a little. Written in the Common script.
dwarvish: The language of dwarves: hard consonants and guttural sounds, carried into their runes and carvings. Script: Dwarvish.
elvish: Fluid and musical, with subtle intonations and intricate grammar; elven literature is rich and widely admired. Script: Elvish.
giant: The booming tongue of giants and ogres, descended from the old Jotun speech. Script: Dwarvish.
gnomish: The language of gnomes, famous for its inventive technical and scholarly treatises. Script: Dwarvish.
goblin: The harsh, rapid speech of goblinoids — goblins, hobgoblins, and bugbears. Script: Dwarvish.
halfling: The warm, homely tongue of halflings — seldom written, passed on in story, recipe, and song. Script: Common.
orc: The brutal, grating language of orcs, full of threats and boasts. Script: Dwarvish.
orcish: The brutal, grating language of orcs, full of threats and boasts. Script: Dwarvish.
abyssal: The chaotic, vile tongue of demons and the Abyss; merely hearing it can unsettle a listener. Script: Infernal.
celestial: The radiant, melodic language of celestials and the Upper Planes. Script: Celestial.
deep speech: The alien, mind-bending language of aberrations — mind flayers, beholders — barely shaped for mortal mouths. It has no script.
draconic: One of the oldest languages, spoken by dragons and dragonborn; much arcane writing is set down in Draconic. Script: Draconic.
infernal: The precise, contractual tongue of devils and the Nine Hells — every word weighed like a clause. Script: Infernal.
primordial: The elemental language, with Auran, Aquan, Ignan, and Terran dialects; one who knows Primordial understands them all.
sylvan: The lilting language of the Feywild — fey, dryads, and many woodland creatures. Script: Elvish.
undercommon: The trade cant of the Underdark, spoken by drow and others who dwell below. Script: Elvish.
auran: The breathy, whistling dialect of Primordial spoken by creatures of the Elemental Plane of Air.
aquan: The flowing, bubbling dialect of Primordial spoken by creatures of the Elemental Plane of Water.
ignan: The crackling, sharp dialect of Primordial spoken by creatures of the Elemental Plane of Fire.
terran: The slow, grinding dialect of Primordial spoken by creatures of the Elemental Plane of Earth.
druidic: The secret language of druids. You can speak it and leave hidden messages; those who know Druidic spot such a message automatically, while others have little chance of noticing it.
thieves' cant: A secret mix of dialect, jargon, and code that hides a message inside seemingly normal conversation. Only another who knows Thieves' Cant understands it.
thieves cant: A secret mix of dialect, jargon, and code that hides a message inside seemingly normal conversation. Only another who knows Thieves' Cant understands it.
